topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent light of the dorm room hummed with an irritating constancy. I was slumped on my bed, the textbook resting forgotten on my chest, my eyelids growing heavy with the weight of the afternoon. A slight shift in the air, a subtle rustle from the desk across the room, caught my attention. I blinked slowly, trying to focus on the pattern of dust motes dancing in the weak sunlight filtering through the blinds. Then I saw it: Liam, my roommate, perched on his chair, his gaze fixed intently on my open journal lying on the nightstand. His finger traced a line of script, slow and deliberate. A cold prickle ran down my spine, a sudden, sharp awareness cutting through the languor that had been settling over me. I didn't move, just stared at the ceiling, my heart beginning a slow, heavy thud against my ribs.

[story 2]

My head felt like it was filled with cotton. Every sentence I tried to form seemed to dissolve before it reached my mouth. I was drifting, the edges of my vision softening into a warm, hazy blur. When I finally managed to crack an eye open, the room looked strangely distorted. Liam was sitting at his desk, a book open in his lap, but his eyes weren't on the page. They were locked onto my journal. A strange, unwelcome tension tightened my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I felt a distinct urge to bolt upright, but my limbs felt weighted, unresponsive. I watched, paralyzed by a strange mix of alarm and utter inertia, as he carefully turned a page, his expression unreadable in the dim light.

[story 3]

The silence in the room was thick, pressing down on my ears. My eyelids felt impossibly heavy, a physical resistance to opening them further. I was leaning against the headboard, my body heavy with an unshakeable need for stillness. It was then I registered the subtle movement from across the room. Liam was there, ostensibly studying, but his attention was entirely diverted to the worn leather-bound book beside my bed. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a flush of unwelcome awareness. A small, involuntary sound escaped my throat—a soft, shaky exhale. I squeezed my eyes shut again, wishing with all my might that the current wave of fatigue would just swallow me whole, leaving me untouched by the unfolding situation.
